summary: anthropic-toolkit postinstall harvests developer identity and cloud/SCM config and POSTs to remote endpoint
details: anthropic-toolkit@1.2.0 presents itself as a TypeScript helper for the Anthropic SDK (declares @anthropic-ai/sdk as a peerDependency, sets main to dist/index.js) but ships no dist/ directory and contains no library code. The only executable content is scripts/postinstall.js, which runs automatically on `npm install`. The postinstall harvests installer-side identity and organizational context: hostname, username, Windows domain (os.hostname/os.userInfo/os.platform), git user.email from ~/.gitconfig and the project's.git/config, GitHub CLI identity from ~/.config/gh/hosts.yml, reflog committer emails, public-key comments from ~/.ssh/*.pub, GCP account/project from ~/.config/gcloud/properties, AWS profile/SSO/account identifiers from ~/.aws/config, DNS search domain, project package.json metadata, and CI identifiers. The collected JSON is sent via https.request POST to a hardcoded endpoint at npm-package-logger-228835561205.europe-west1.run.app. The package is framed as anonymous telemetry, but the explicit collection of personal/corporate identity and SCM/cloud account context — combined with the absence of any actual library functionality and the impersonation of the Anthropic SDK namespace — is supply-chain reconnaissance against installers, not telemetry.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
